gaps: A way to pull the target case's own opinion-cluster text or a case summary from the corpus — the docket links one cluster but its contents are unreachable on-runner, so I could not tell which of the case's multiple historical appearances this docket represents
fedcourts query rows mostly lack case_name/date_filed/topic, which makes it hard to judge whether a prior is actually similar
A documented disposition-label mapping for historical merits cases (affirmed/reversed -> other?) to remove label-choice guesswork
helpful: fedcourts stats --court scotus: the disposition split (78% 'other') directly determined the predicted_disposition label
fedcourts paths: confirmed resolved output locations
fedcourts stats --group-by term_year: showed resolved outcomes are almost all undated historical events, confirming the base rate is drawn from cases like this one
notes: Base-rate stats were again the decisive tool for this bare historical docket; query returned rows with too little metadata to assess similarity, so it contributed nothing case-specific.
